title: Linked Worktrees and Parallel Branch Operations · History Workshop
summary: A pairing session predicts each repository-state transition before building the first multi-worktree ownership and cleanup map for linked worktrees and parallel branch operations. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of multi-worktree ownership and cleanup map while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish common repository data from per-worktree HEAD, index, refs, operation state, config, and administrative links.
Add branch, detached, orphan, and relative-path worktrees; then lock, unlock, repair, and move them with path and device constraints.
Reason about checked-out branch exclusivity, shared refs, concurrent fetch or maintenance, and safe cross-worktree branch deletion and rewrite.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Case G73 is a workshop in a disposable repository. a stale administrative entry is locking the release branch after its directory moved. The learner must predict porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state, run git worktree list --porcelain, explain the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason, test this changed condition: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision, and preserve this recovery boundary: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish common repository data from per-worktree head, index, refs, operation state, config, and administrative links.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-parallel-operation-policy-1vropp" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Parallel Operation Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-parallel-operation-policy-1vropp
# scenario-key: trace-1vropp probe-pporv1 boundary-ropp1v owner-1vroppx fault-1vroppz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1v candidate=b00b1pp
# hypothesis: a stale administrative entry is locking the release branch after its directory moved
git worktree list --porcelain
# expected: porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state
# observation: the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason
# changed-case: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision
# after-ref: main=a11ce1v candidate=c0ffeep
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair
# transfer-limit: worktrees share refs and objects, so concurrent real maintenance and rewrites need coordination
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
